title: Furniture Placement System
prompt:
Use Three.js to implement an interactive room furniture placement system that supports drag-and-drop from a catalog, move/rotate, collision detection, grid snapping, and layout saving.

[Three.js Import (must follow strictly)]
Add the following importmap in <head>, and import via ES Module in <script type="module">:
<script type="importmap">
{ "imports": {
    "three": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/build/three.module.js",
    "three/addons/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/examples/jsm/"
} }
</script>
<script type="module">
import * as THREE from 'three';
import { OrbitControls } from 'three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js';
</script>

[Room]
(1) Room dimensions: 10m(X) x 8m(Z), wall height 3m(Y).
(2) Floor: PlaneGeometry(10, 8), y=0, light wood color #d4a373 (MeshStandardMaterial, roughness=0.8).
(3) 4 walls: thin BoxGeometry walls (thickness=0.1), white material, enclosing the room on all four sides. Wall y range [0, 3].
(4) No ceiling (top is open so the interior is visible from above).

[Furniture Catalog (6 procedural furniture types, all using basic geometry combinations)]
Each furniture item is a Group containing multiple Meshes:

(5) Sofa: rectangular seat (2x0.4x0.8) + backrest (2x0.6x0.15), brown #8B4513. AABB ~2x1x0.8.
(6) Table: tabletop (1.2x0.05x0.8) + 4 legs (0.05x0.7x0.05), light brown #DEB887. AABB ~1.2x0.75x0.8.
(7) Chair: seat (0.5x0.05x0.5) + 4 legs (0.05x0.45x0.05) + backrest (0.5x0.5x0.05), dark brown #654321. AABB ~0.5x0.9x0.5.
(8) Lamp: base cylinder (r=0.15, h=0.05) + pole (r=0.03, h=0.6) + shade (ConeGeometry r=0.2, h=0.25), off-white #FAEBD7. AABB ~0.4x0.85x0.4. Add PointLight (yellow, intensity 0.5, distance=3) at shade position.
(9) Bookshelf: frame (0.8x1.5x0.3) assembled from multiple BoxGeometries into 4 shelves, dark wood #5C3317. AABB ~0.8x1.5x0.3.
(10) Bed: mattress (2x0.3x1.5) + bed frame (2.1x0.4x1.6) + headboard (0.05x0.8x1.5), light blue mattress #B0C4DE + wood frame #8B7355. AABB ~2.1x0.8x1.6.

[Furniture Catalog Panel]
(11) id="catalog": HTML panel containing 6 clickable items, each showing the furniture name.
  Each item's data-type attribute is "sofa", "table", "chair", "lamp", "bookshelf", or "bed".
(12) Clicking a catalog item enters placement mode: clicking the floor places the corresponding furniture at that position.

[Placing Furniture]
(13) Click the floor (Raycaster detects the floor Mesh) to place furniture at the hit point.
(14) Furniture bottom aligned to floor (placed at y=0, furniture AABB lowest point at y=0).
(15) Placement mode exits automatically after placing.

[Selection and Movement]
(16) Click a placed furniture item: select it (highlight outline or change color), show selection indicator.
(17) Selected furniture can be dragged to move (mousedown + mousemove + mouseup on ground plane); furniture follows mouse on the floor.
(18) Movement constrained to room floor area (furniture AABB must not exceed wall boundaries).

[Rotation]
(19) Press R key: rotate the currently selected furniture by 90 degrees (around Y axis). Each R press rotates Math.PI/2.

[Delete]
(20) Press Delete key or Backspace key: delete the currently selected furniture.

[AABB Collision Detection]
(21) When placing or moving furniture, check if the new position collides with existing furniture using AABB.
(22) If collision, reject placement/movement (furniture returns to original position or is not placed); do not update position.
(23) AABB collision detection uses THREE.Box3 to compute bounding boxes for each furniture item.

[Snap to Grid]
(24) id="snapToggle": checkbox, off by default.
(25) When enabled, furniture positions snap to a 0.5m grid (x and z coordinates rounded to nearest 0.5m multiple).

[Save/Load]
(26) id="saveBtn": save the current layout to localStorage (key="furnitureLayout") in JSON format containing each furniture item's type, position, and rotation.
(27) id="loadBtn": read from localStorage and restore the layout.

[Lighting and Camera]
- DirectionalLight white from (5, 10, 5), intensity 1.0, castShadow enabled.
- AmbientLight intensity 0.4.
- OrbitControls, initial position looking down at 45 degrees from above.

[State Exposure (required, update after operations)]
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  furnitureCount: number,         // number of currently placed furniture items
  furniture: [{                   // info for each furniture item
    type: string,                 // "sofa"|"table"|"chair"|"lamp"|"bookshelf"|"bed"
    x: number, y: number, z: number,  // position
    rotation: number              // Y-axis rotation in radians
  }, ...],
  selectedIndex: number | null,   // index of currently selected furniture (null = none)
  snapEnabled: boolean,           // whether grid snap is enabled
  roomSize: { w: 10, h: 8 }       // room dimensions
}

Output a single self-contained HTML file that runs directly in the browser.
physics_constraints: 6 procedural furniture types with specific dimensions and AABB
AABB collision detection: overlapping placement rejected
Room boundary: furniture AABB must stay within walls
Snap to grid: positions round to 0.5m multiples when enabled
R key rotates selected furniture by exactly pi/2 radians
Save/Load via localStorage with full layout restore
